title: Dynamic values in docs - mirror column

posts.author: ArturPM (Artur Gula)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!

I’m checking a new feature with Dynamic values in docs, and I found some issue with mirror columns.

I created a column that summarizes expected work from all the linked tasks.

But when I add this column to the document, then it displays all the single values instead of the sum. So I see the values like: 10, 20, 10 instead of one value = 40.

Is it an expected behavior?
Is there any workaround for this? I tried ot use a formula column, but it is not supported in dynamic values yet.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Artur,

Thank you for flagging this!

Given this is one of our new features, I will take this with the dedicated team internally and get back to you on expected behaviour as soon as possible.

I appreciate your patience with me

posts.author: JanieW (Janie Walton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a similar issue with a mirror column and dynamic value @ArturPM . I have raised this with support and they are also adding as a feature request. My issue is I am using a dynamic value for a mirrored dropdown column which pulls required resources from 3 items. The board shows unique values:

but the monday doc shows the duplicates which obviously doesn’t look great:

I need it to only display unique values, like the board does.

Also any possibility of being able to change the format (colour or background) or dynamic values. Think this might be a little trickier.

Thanks!
